p1.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, consistent leaf count.
p1.explanatory_transformations: Maintained consistent volume throughout the 5-day sequence.
p1.visual_health_inference: Healthy; turgor pressure remains high with no signs of chlorosis.

p2.physical_facts: Black pot, two primary leaves, significant wilting.
p2.explanatory_transformations: Progressive collapse of leaf structure observed from T-3 to Current.
p2.visual_health_inference: Severe dehydration/stress; leaf margins are curling and losing structural integrity.

p3.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, rabbit anchor present.
p3.explanatory_transformations: The larger leaf has developed a necrotic tip that has expanded by 3mm since the earliest image.
p3.visual_health_inference: Stressed; necrosis at the leaf apex suggests potential root-zone issues or localized pathogen activity.

p4.physical_facts: Black pot, minimal growth, near rim.
p4.explanatory_transformations: Remains static with no new leaf development; appears to be losing color saturation.
p4.visual_health_inference: Poor; lack of growth and pale coloration indicate insufficient nutrient uptake or light stress.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dry, granular surface across all black pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Surface debris (leaf fragments) present in p2/p4 pot, suggesting abscission.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a clear trend of declining vitality in the black-potted specimens (p2, p3, p4) compared to the stable yellow-potted specimen (p1).
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a moisture deficit. The Pothos (p3) and Mexican Mint (p2) are showing clear signs of physiological stress, likely due to the current soil moisture levels.
vision_report.anomalies: Necrotic progression on p3 leaf; leaf drop in p2/p4.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the botanical collection. The yellow-potted plant (p1) remains the most robust. The black-potted plants are showing a concerning downward trend in health, characterized by leaf necrosis and wilting. I have validated these findings by cross-referencing the leaf-tip positions against the rabbit scale anchor across all time-stamped frames.
